observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:


reconcile the records per journals and per ledger to identify the discrepancies and make necessary adjustment to disclose the correct balance of the account; and 

to carefully review the posting of entries in the ledger to ensure the correctness of the amounts.
observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile and identify the discrepancies and make the necessary adjusting entries.
observations.reason: The Municipal Accountant is currently reconciling the said journal and accounts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;

create new positions and provide capable personnel that will be in-charge with the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and the monitoring of the delinquent taxpayers; and intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.
observations.managementAction:
Conduct massive tax campaign and intensify revenue collection activities thru service caravan to all barangay

Appraise & assess the infrastructure improvements of every household and establishments in the Municipality of Lupi.

Send demand letters to all delinquent Real Property Taxpayers
observations.reason: The Management opted not to implement the recommendation since Officials from far flung Barangay’s will have a hard time depositing the said receipts.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the conduct of complete annual physical count of Inventory in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Vol.1; 

Require the Accountant and GSO to regularly maintain and update the SLC and SC, respectively; and after the conduct of annual physical count, require the reconciliation of records of the GSO and Accounting. Likewise, reconciliation of RPCI with SC and SLC shall be made.
observations.reason:
The management failed to create the Inventory Committee when the chairman of the committee retired on August 31, 2021.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require the: 

Supply Officer to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs using the prescribed format and maintain individual property card for all PPEs owned by the LGU;

Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete PPELC to ensure proper monitoring and include PPEs acquired in CYs 2019 to 2020 in their updating of the PPE Schedule; and 

Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile noted discrepancies in the RPCPPE and PPE balances.
observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal accountant to maintain and update PPE ledger cards and the Inventory committee should conduct physical count of PPEs in order to facilitate reconciliation of records.
observations.reason:
Concerned offices failed to conduct physical count of inventories and the accounting has no PPE Ledger cards.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.managementAction: Direct the Municipal Account and the Municipal Engineer to conduct inventory of road networks and reconcile the existing records.
observations.reason: Incomplete records in the accounting office and failure of the Inventory Committee to conduct physical count precluded Management from implementing the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to take-up or adjust in the books subject parcels of lots which were officially received by the LGU as government property at acquisition cost or fair market value in pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17 and Section 63 of PD 1445;

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Custodian to maintain updated report of the LGU’s properties; and

Require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate the registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title of the aforementioned parcels of lots in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds.
observations.managementAction: The Management is continuously getting update of the approval of the land survey in the Bureau of Lands.
observations.reason:
Value of the parcels of land has yet to be established.

Physical count of the PPE is not yet complete.

Delay in securing the necessary documents for registration/ transfer of title of the subject parcel of land was due to the pending approval of land survey from the Bureau of Lands.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to complete the BRS and record the appropriate adjusting journal entries to show the accurate balance of the cash in bank account after taking into consideration the necessary supporting documents and computations, if any, as bases in preparing the adjusting journal entries, in conformity with Section 3.3 of COA Circular No. 96-011; and
Require the Municipal Accountant to identify and include in the BRS the specific Journal Vouchers No. of all valid reconciling items that require adjustment and correction in the General Ledger.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to take up in the books the fund received and record it as Trust liability and maintain a separate subsidiary ledger for the same.

Recommendation:
We recommended and the Management agreed to: 

Require the Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Chapter 7, Vol. 1 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs’ with regard to the proper Accounting entry in the books for Inventory Accounts; and 
Make the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to reflect the correct balances of Inventory.

observations.managementAction: In September 2020 the LGU conducted disposal of unserviceable properties worth ₱7,455,000.00 with net book value of ₱1,682,944.45 and generated cash inflow of ₱856,075.00
observations.reason:
Management is still in the process of identifying assets that are obsolete and unserviceable.

Updating of IIRUP and Identification of missing PPEs will be done when the LGU implement the one-time cleansing of PPE  count.

Interview/ Inquiries with the  drivers of dump trucks showed that diligence were exerted in using these equipment, but due to the non-availability of its spare parts, they found hard to maintain the good running condition of the unit.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation
We recommended and the Management agreed to:

Designate Property/Supply t staff  to be in charge of monitoring procured DRRM items; 

Require Property/Supply Unit staff to prepare Inventory List of all DRRM items, including PPEs and Supplies; and

Submit monthly Report of Supplies and Material Issued (RSMI) to Accounting unit for recording in the books.
observations.managementAction: The Head of the DRRM is the designated Property/Supply Unit Staff
observations.reason: Most items procured were delivered off site and issued outright precluding the responsible officer from preparing the required documents/ reports.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties of the Municipal Government and prepare and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team as mandated by the above provision; and

Require the Accountant and the MGSO/Property Officer to maintain the prescribed PPELCs and PCs, respectively and reconcile them with the subsidiary ledgers and Report on the Physical Count of the PPE (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: Management conducted actual physical count of movable properties only.
observations.reason:
Physical count of PPEs was not completed due to voluminous workload of personnel involved in the inventory taking and the inadequacy of the number of personnel to handle/maintain ledger cards.

Restated in Part II of this Report.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to observe more prudence in the use of government funds and refrain from incurring extravagant expenditures and consider cost efficient options in the conduct of workshops and other activities of the Municipality to avoid exceptions in audit.  We likewise recommended that Management submit the duly approved training proposal and Capacity Development Agenda of the Municipality for further evaluation and audit to forestall the issuance of Notices of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Disallowance was issued by the Audit Team Leader which was not appealed were paid by some of the outgoing LGU Officials.
observations.reason: Most of the Officials subject of Notices of Disallowance have already paid their respective liabilities thru salary deduction but proof of payment has yet to be presented to the audit team.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Management continued to monitor the progress of the on-going projects in order to insure compliance upon completion.
observations.reason: Infrastructure projects for CY 2019 were implemented, but some incurred delays due to the pandemic.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

coordinate with the concerned agencies and personnel to facilitate the immediate utilization of the Community Fish Landing Center so as not to deprive beneficiaries of the beneficial use of the tangible assets; and

ensure that the movable assets are properly kept in a secured room or building to lessen their exposure to too much sunlight or rain and avoid the risk of loss and misuse.
observations.managementAction: Public hearing was already conducted.
observations.reason: Construction of Community Fish landing was completed but not yet operational due to the absence of electricity and water connection.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require:

All undertakings entered into by the Municipality with  source agencies for the implementation of latter’s projects be supported with a corresponding Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and 

Specific guidelines for the project implementation and utilization of the fund be formulated to guarantee that only valid, proper and correct disbursements are undertaken by the Municipality.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to gather all the details and information needed to be able to record as PPE each item in the RPIs. 

We likewise recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to thereafter, take up the necessary journal entries to recognize in the books public infrastructure assets totaling ₱25,685,499.82, as well as the related accumulated depreciation and impairment losses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004, to present fairly the value of the assets in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction:
The Management prioritized the reconciliation of records and make necessary adjusting entries.

The Municipal Accountant is still in the process of taking up the necessary journal entries.
observations.reason:
Reconciliation of RPIs and Municipal Accountant’s schedule of PPE is still in progress.

The reason for delay is the voluminous items in the record.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the liquidation of fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects and subsequently require for the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant already prepared and submitted the partial liquidation reports to the concerned NGAs.
observations.reason: The projects are not yet fully implemented by the concerned department but partial liquidations have been submitted.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;

intensify the tax campaign by assigning and sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time; and

conduct regular assessment and reassessment of newly constructed and improved properties.
observations.managementAction: The Management has collected only ₱1,513,853.84 or 71.83% of the total estimated collections in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to implement the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require: (a) the use of duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket, preparation of Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as prescribed by regulations; and (b) on a monthly basis, the submission to the Auditor of the Report of Official Travel and Report of Fuel Consumption of all its machineries and equipment, including those equipment used for infrastructure projects.

We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims for fuel consumption if not duly supported with the documents prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.managementAction: The Management is undergoing assessment of procurement process.
observations.reason: Preparation of Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were not religiously observed.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:

Adopt measures to intensify revenue collection efforts by strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the Municipality and conduct periodic assessment/evaluation of collection accomplishments in order to ensure that all sources are tapped to maximize the revenue generating powers mandated by law; and

Conduct study of the present collection, recording and monitoring system in the Municipal Treasurer Office and formulate and adopt the most efficient, effective and economy measures to improve the LGU’s collection system and revenue generation.
observations.managementAction: The Management has collected only ₱1,513,853.84 or 71.83% of the total estimated collections in CY 2020.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to implement the recommendations.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to:
reconcile the records per journals and per ledger to identify the discrepancies and make necessary adjustment to disclose the correct balance of the account; and
to carefully review the posting of entries in the ledger to ensure the correctness of the amounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;
create new positions and provide capable personnel that will be in-charge with the issuance of Notice of Delinquency and the monitoring of the delinquent taxpayers; and
intensify the tax campaign by sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time.

Require the conduct of complete annual physical count of Inventory in accordance with Section 124 of the Manual on the NGAS for LGUs, Vol.1;
Require the Accountant and GSO to regularly maintain and update the SLC and SC, respectively; and
After the conduct of annual physical count, require the reconciliation of records of the GSO and Accounting. Likewise, reconciliation of RPCI with SC and SLC shall be made.

Require the Accountant to strictly adhere to the provisions of Chapter 7, Vol. 1 of Manual on the NGAS for LGUs’ with regard to the proper Accounting entry in the books for Inventory Accounts; and
Make the necessary adjustments in the accounting books to reflect the correct balances of Inventory.

Supply Officer to conduct a complete physical count of all PPEs using the prescribed format and maintain individual property card for all PPEs owned by the LGU;
Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete PPELC to ensure proper monitoring and include PPEs acquired in CYs 2019 to 2020 in their updating of the PPE Schedule; and
Supply Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile noted discrepancies in the RPCPPE and PPE balances.

Designate Property/Supply Unit staff  to be in charge of monitoring procured DRRM items;
Require Property/Supply Unit staff to prepare Inventory List of all DRRM items, including PPEs and Supplies; and
Submit monthly Report of Supplies and Material Issued (RSMI) to Accounting unit for recording in the books.

We recommended and the Management agreed to:
Strictly follow the LGU-NGAS Manual in accounting for Special Funds; and
Require the Accountant to maintain separate book of accounts for Trust Funds pertaining to the abovementioned transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: The Management continued to monitor the progress of the on-going projects in order to insure compliance upon completion.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

require the GAD focal group to judiciously plan the GAD programs and projects in order to adequately achieve the objectives of the fund;

implement the programs and projects covered by GAD annual plan and budget to achieve its objective; and

reinforce the manpower involved in the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The Management continued the planned activities while observing the health protocols.
observations.reason: GAD programs, projects and activities were delayed because of the restrictions imposed during Enhanced Community Quarantine, and the MSWDO was overwhelmed in the relief efforts after the Bicol Region was hit by three consecutive typhoons in November and December.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe proper charging against the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (LDRRM) Fund in accordance to what is mandated by the IRR of R.A. 10121.

observations.managementAction:
The Management had already switched to sanitary landfill.


The Construction of Sanitary Landfill is on-going.


The Management had already contracted a trainer for the staff.



The Management already adopted a scheduling scheme but not yet implemented because the training of staff is yet to be conducted.
observations.reason:
Training of staff is yet to be conducted.




Training of staff is yet to be conducted.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the procurement activities of the Municipality be properly planned and acquisitions of supplies, equipment, construction materials, etc., be in consonance with the mandate of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
	
We also recommended and Management agreed to refrain from disbursing public funds which are not in accordance with laws, rules and regulations as to avoid exceptions in audit.

Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to practice diligence in recording transactions, ensure that transactions are recorded in their proper accounts and make the necessary correcting entries, if applicable.
observations.managementAction: The Management is undergoing assessment of procurement process.
observations.reason: There are transactions which are processed as the need arises.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to initiate appropriate steps in order to augment the staff deficiencies in the LDRRMO as cited under item 5.5 of Joint Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Management already appointed a permanent MDRRMO Head and staff.

observations.recommendation:
Recommendation:
The Municipal Accountant is enjoined and agreed to effect the transfer of unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment with total cost of ₱6,162,982.00 to Other Assets account.  Likewise, the Municipality is required to create a committee or body to undertake the disposition of all unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Disposal of unserviceable assets was undertaken in CY 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

re-establish and reinforce the deputation of barangay officials to aid the LGU in collecting taxes by setting their specific functions and responsibilities;

intensify the tax campaign by assigning and sending treasury personnel to barangays especially during barangay assemblies for tax information drive in order to invite more taxpayers to pay taxes on time; and

conduct regular assessment and reassessment of newly constructed and improved properties.
observations.reason: Lack of manpower to implement the recommendations.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

require the GAD focal group to judiciously plan the GAD programs and projects in order to adequately achieve the objectives of the fund;

implement the programs and projects covered by GAD annual plan and budget to achieve its objective; and

reinforce the manpower involved in the implementation of GAD related programs, projects and activities.
observations.reason: GAD PPAs were not aligned with the needs of the targeted beneficiaries thus implementation of GAD Plan was ineffective.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strictly observe proper charging against the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management (LDRRM) Fund in accordance to what is mandated by the IRR of R.A. 10121.
observations.reason: Internet and Telephone expenses related to MDRRM Office are still being charged to the DRRM Fund per Accounting’s record.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to require: (a) the use of duly accomplished Driver’s Trip Ticket, preparation of Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as prescribed by regulations; and (b) on a monthly basis, the submission to the Auditor of the Report of Official Travel and Report of Fuel Consumption of all its machineries and equipment, including those equipment used for infrastructure projects.

We also recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to refrain from processing claims for fuel consumption if not duly supported with the documents prescribed in COA Circular No. 77-61.
observations.reason: Preparation of Monthly Report of Official Travel and Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption were not religiously observed.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the procurement activities of the Municipality be properly planned and acquisitions of supplies, equipment, construction materials, etc., be in consonance with the mandate of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
	
We also recommended and Management agreed to refrain from disbursing public funds which are not in accordance with laws, rules and regulations as to avoid exceptions in audit.

Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to practice diligence in recording transactions, ensure that transactions are recorded in their proper accounts and make the necessary correcting entries, if applicable.
observations.reason: Purchase of gasoline was not consolidated, instead the Management adopted Small Value Procurement. They explained that the procurement process is only undertaken once the supplier sends the bill.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the Municipal Accountant to take-up or adjust in the books subject parcels of lots which were officially received by the LGU as government property at acquisition cost or fair market value in pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17 and Section 63 of PD 1445;
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Custodian to maintain updated report of the LGU’s properties; and
Require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate the registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title of the aforementioned parcels of lots in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, to gather all the details and information needed to be able to record as PPE each item in the RPIs.
We likewise recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to thereafter, take up the necessary journal entries to recognize in the books public infrastructure assets totaling ₱25,685,499.82, as well as the related accumulated depreciation and impairment losses, if any, in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2016-004, to present fairly the value of the assets in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Create an Inventory Committee composed of regular employees who shall, on a full-time basis and prescribed timeline, conduct the physical inventory of all properties and prepare and submit the corresponding reports; and
Designate one personnel each from the Offices of the Accountant and the Property Officer who shall maintain the prescribed PPELCs and PCs and reconcile them with the subsidiary ledgers and Report on the Physical Count of the PPE (RPCPPE).

We recommended and Management agreed to require the liquidation of fund transfers once projects are fully implemented and/or refund the unutilized amount from completed projects and subsequently require for the appropriate recording in the books of accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
require the GAD system in-charge to judiciously plan the GAD programs and projects in order to properly utilize the budget;
implement the programs and projects covered by GAD Annual Plan and Budget to achieve its objectives; and
require all units involved in the implementation of GAD related PPAs to ensure proper coordination to avoid duplication of projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Adopt measures to intensify revenue collection efforts by strictly enforcing existing tax laws and ordinances within the Municipality and conduct periodic assessment/evaluation of collection accomplishments in order to ensure that all sources are tapped to maximize the revenue generating powers mandated by law; and
Conduct study of the present collection, recording and monitoring system in the Municipal Treasurer Office and formulate and adopt the most efficient, effective and economy measures to improve the LGU’s collection system and revenue generation.

We recommended and Management agreed to direct the Municipal Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books and to make the necessary cancellation and/or adjustment of stale checks to restore the cash equivalent to the appropriate Cash in Bank - LCCA accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared the necessary adjustments.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory of all properties of the Municipal Government and prepare and submit the corresponding reports to the Audit Team as mandated by the above provision; and

Require the Accountant and the MGSO/Property Officer to maintain the prescribed PPELCs and PCs, respectively and reconcile them with the subsidiary ledgers and Report on the Physical Count of the PPE (RPCPPE).
observations.managementAction: Management conducted actual physical count of PPE but was not completed.
observations.reason: Physical count of PPEs was not completed due to voluminous workload of personnel involved in the inventory taking and the inadequacy of the number of personnel to handle/maintain ledger cards.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

Require the Municipal Accountant to take-up or adjust in the books subject parcels of lots which were officially received by the LGU as government property at acquisition cost or fair market value in pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17 and Section 63 of PD 1445;

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Custodian to maintain updated report of the LGU’s properties; and

Require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate the registration of the untitled land and/or transfer of title of the aforementioned parcels of lots in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds.
observations.managementAction: Management applied for land titling.
observations.reason:
Adjustment in the Books cannot be effected due to lack of supporting documents.

Restated in Part II

We likewise recommended that Management submit the duly approved training proposal and Capacity Development Agenda of the Municipality for further evaluation and audit to forestall the issuance of Notices of Disallowance.
observations.managementAction: Notice of Disallowance was issued by the Audit Team Leader which was not appealed were paid by some of the outgoing LGU Officials.
observations.reason: Most of the Officials subject of Notices of Disallowance are not yet paying their respective liabilities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to strategically implement its developmental projects within the target period of accomplishment to ensure that the 20% of the IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio-economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2017-1.
observations.managementAction: Management implemented eight PPAs and reprogrammed seven PPAs funded by the 20% EDF of Prior years.
observations.reason: LGU’s trucks and pay loader broke down and is currently under repair which further delayed the implementation of PPAs that are by Administration.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to:

coordinate with the concerned agencies and personnel to facilitate the immediate utilization of the Community Fish Landing Center so as not to deprive beneficiaries of the beneficial use of the tangible assets; and

ensure that the movable assets are properly kept in a secured room or building to lessen their exposure to too much sunlight or rain and avoid the risk of loss and misuse.
observations.managementAction: Management prepared and submitted the Operations Manual to Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: The Sanggunaiag Bayan has yet to enact an S.B. Ordinance which would guide the operation of the Community Fish Landing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the creation of a functional LCPC and ensure proper coordination and assistance in the adoption and implementation of projects, plans, and activities geared toward child protection and the development of a child-friendly environment.

We recommended and Management agreed to require:

All undertakings entered into by the Municipality with  source agencies for the implementation of latter’s projects be supported with a corresponding Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and 

Specific guidelines for the project implementation and utilization of the fund be formulated to guarantee that only valid, proper and correct disbursements are undertaken by the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Some of the MOA of the projects were submitted to the Auditor’s Office.
observations.reason: The Source Agency failed to provide a signed copy of the MOA to the Implementing Agency.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to ensure the timely implementation of BuB projects so as not to deprive the constituents of the Municipality of the beneficial use of the projects.

We recommended and Management agreed to:

ensure that payments are made directly to suppliers/creditors in the form of checks and only petty operating expenses should be reimbursed through the Petty Cash Fund; 

require that all procurements be properly planned depending on the available budget in order to maintain the normal flow of cash; and 

direct the payment as obligations fall due without resorting to advances from personal funds of officials and employees.
observations.managementAction: Management established petty cash fund.

The closure plan was already submitted to the DENR awaiting approval. Site development planning for the sanitary landfill is ongoing with a Consultant and is close to coming up with the final output.

MRF structure construction was completed.

There is still no segregation at the household level.



With regular waste collection at Poblacion, Colacling, Sooc, Bel-Cruz, and Lourdes.
observations.reason: Implementation of the recommendations is dependent on the completion of the Site Development Plan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to initiate appropriate steps in order to augment the staff deficiencies in the LDRRMO as cited under item 5.5 of Joint Memorandum Circular.
observations.managementAction: The Management already appointed a permanent MDRRMO Head.
observations.reason: Personnel under the MDRRM Office has yet to comply with the minimum requirement/ qualifications.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant is enjoined and agreed to effect the transfer of unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment with total cost of ₱6,162,982.00 to Other Assets account.  Likewise, the Municipality is required to create a committee or body to undertake the disposition of all unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: Management created Disposal Committee.
observations.reason: No disposal of unserviceable assets was conducted by the Disposal Committee due to time constraints and lack of manpower.

observations.recommendation: For a systematic and effective review of transactions, we recommended and Management agreed that copies of Purchase Orders, together with its supporting documents, be transmitted to the Audit Team within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-010.

Strict compliance with aforementioned rules and regulations is highly enjoined.  To ensure prompt inspection of deliveries, agency official responsible for accepting and inspecting deliveries should, within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance/ inspection, notify the auditor of the time and date of scheduled deliveries.

We recommended and Management agreed to do the following:

The Municipal Budget Officer is enjoined to incorporate in the budget for Personal Services the amount/rate of the hazard allowance as prescribed in R.A. No. 7305 and COA Circular No. 2013-003; 

The Municipal Health Officer is required to refund the excess hazard allowance of ₱29,107.50; and 

Henceforth, payment of hazard allowance should be in accordance with the rate set forth in R.A. No. 7305 and COA Circular No. 2013-003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Health Officer filed an appeal to the Commission. Said appeal was granted in favour of the appellant on June 14, 2019 under COA RO 5 Decision No. 2019-07.

observations.recommendation:
We  recommended  and  Management  agreed  to  direct   the   Municipal
Accountant to effect the necessary adjustments in the books and to make  the
necessary cancellation and/or adjustment of  stale  checks  to  restore  the
cash equivalent to the appropriate Cash in Bank - LCCA accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed to:


   a. Require the Inventory Committee to conduct physical inventory  of  all
      properties of the Municipal Government  and  prepare  and  submit  the
      corresponding reports to the Audit  Team  as  mandated  by  the  above
      provision; and


   b. Require the Accountant and the MGSO/Property Officer to  maintain  the
      prescribed PPELCs and PCs, respectively and reconcile  them  with  the
      subsidiary ledgers and  Report  on  the  Physical  Count  of  the  PPE
      (RPCPPE).

We recommended and Management agreed:

   a) Require the Municipal Accountant to take-up or  adjust  in  the  books
      subject parcels of lots which were officially received by the  LGU  as
      government property at  acquisition  cost  or  fair  market  value  in
      pursuant to Sections 26 and 27 of PPSAS 17 and Section 63 of PD 1445;


   b) Instruct the Municipal Treasurer as the Property Custodian to maintain
      updated report of the LGU’s properties; and


   c) Require the Municipal Assessor’s Office to facilitate the registration
      of the untitled land and/or transfer of title  of  the  aforementioned
      parcels of lots in the name of the LGU with the Registry of Deeds.

We recommended and Management agreed to observe more prudence  in  the
use of government funds and refrain from incurring extravagant  expenditures
and consider cost efficient options in the conduct of workshops and other
activities of the Municipality to avoid exceptions in  audit.   We  likewise
recommended that Management submit the duly approved training  proposal  and
Capacity Development Agenda of the Municipality for further  evaluation  and
audit to forestall the issuance of Notices of Disallowance.

be investigated

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to:
start the process and
implement appropriate
steps that would lead
to the closure of the
controlled dump and
would result to
switching to sanitary
landfill;
intensify efforts to
achieve and embrace
the full concept of
Ecological Solid
Waste Management and
adhere to the
provisions of R.A.
9003;
discontinue the
practice of collecting
non-segregated waste
and come up with
measures that would
help its cause.
Encouraging waste
segregation at source
will also be
beneficial in
transitioning from an
open dumpsite to a
sanitary land fill as it
would lessen the
amount of wastes that
would go to this
dumpsite; and
adopt scheduling
method/scheme which
would allow the
collection of different
types of wastes on
different days.

We also recommended
and Management agreed
that the ESWM Board
speeds up the
creation of their new
ESWM Plan to adapt to
the current needs and
demands in addressing
environmental
problems.
observations.managementAction:
MRF structure is currently being
constructed.
observations.reason:
Awaiting completion of MRF
construction and operation.

that transactions are
recorded in their
proper accounts and
make the necessary
correcting entries, if
applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to fully —implement —all projects embodied in the Annual and Supplemental Budget and funded under the 20% Local Development Fund during the year the projects are planned to be executed in order that the constituents of the Municipality can immediately benefit from the projects.
observations.managementAction: Implementation of 7 projects. Restated in Part I of this year’s report.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed to: a. start the process and implement appropriate steps that would lead to the closure of the controlled dump and would result to switching to sanitary dumpsites; b. intensify efforts to achieve and embrace the full concept of Ecological Solid Waste Management and adhere to the provisions of RA. 9003; c. discontinue the practice of collecting non-segregated waste and come up with measures that would help its cause. Encouraging waste segregation at source will also be beneficial in transitioning from an open dumpsite to a sanitary land fill as it would lessen the amount of wastes that would go to this dumpsite; and d. adopt scheduling method/scheme which would allow the collection of different types of wastes on different days.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the ESWM Board speeds up the creation of their new ESWM Plan to adapt to the current needs and demands in addressing environmental problems.
observations.managementAction: Wastes collected were segregated before collection.
observations.reason: Inadequacy of fund to implement the closure of dumpsites.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and Management agreed that the procurement activities of the Municipality be properly planned and acquisitions of supplies, equipment, construction materials, etc., be in consonance with the mandate of the Revised IRR of RA No. 9184.
We also recommended and Management agreed to refrain from disbursing public funds which are not in accordance with laws, rules and regulations as to avoid exceptions in audit.
Lastly, we recommended and Management agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to practice diligence in recording transactions, ensure that transactions are recorded in their proper accounts and make the necessary correcting entries, if applicable.

We recommt‘ended‘ and Management agreed to: (a) ensure that payments are

observations.recommendation:
We recommended and
Management agreed to:
a. refrain  from the
procurement of goods/supplies  as
stockpiling measures in the absence/existence of possible/impending hazard event;
b. avoid wastage, we recommend the utilization of soon-to-expire medicines by health centers while Food for Work Program can be adopted to hasten the distribution of soon-to-perish rice; and
c. discontinue the requisitions, a practice which was not in consonance with the mandates of the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (RIRR) of Republic Act (RA) No. 9184, hence, prices were not competitive signifying poor procurement planning,
observations.managementAction: The Management agreed to: a. refrain from the procurement of goods/supplies as stockpiling measures in the absence/existence of possible/impending hazard event; b. avoid wastage, utilize soon-to-expire medicines by health centers and adopt Food for Work Program to hasten distribution of soon-to-perish rice; and c. discontinue requisitions not in consonance with RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.reason: Lack of personnel to practice diligence in recording transactions, ensure that transactions are recorded in their proper accounts and make the necessary correcting entries, if applicable.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the procurement activities _ of  the Municipality be made properly planned and consolidated monthly.
observations.managementAction: The procurement activities of the Municipality be made properly planned and procurement will be made on a monthly basis.
observations.reason: For CY 2017, majority of requisitions will be consolidated monthly and procurement will be made on a monthly basis.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to require the OIC-Municipal Accountant to practice diligence in recording transactions, ensure that transactions are recorded in their proper accounts and make the necessary correcting entries, if applicable.
observations.managementAction: The OIC-Municipal Accountant was directed to practice diligence in recording transactions, ensure that transactions are recorded in their proper accounts and make the necessary correcting entries, if applicable.
observations.reason: LDRRMO was not compliant with the provisions set forth under the NDRRMC, DILG, DBM & CSC Joint Memorandum Circular (MC) No. 2014-01, compromising the capacity of the LDRRMO to carry out its functions, thus, may affect their disaster risk reduction and management programs/projects and activities.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to initiate appropriate steps in order to augment the plantilla staff deficiencies in the LDRRMO.
observations.managementAction: Inclusion of additional personnel in the plantilla.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We  reiterated  our CY previous years’ 2015 recommendation and Management agreed to regularly conduct the required inventory-taking, reconcile inventory reports with accounting records and submit the same to the Audit Team with existing rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: The Management prepared Report on the Physical Count of Inventories.
observations.reason: The personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed to fully implement all projects embodied in the Annual Investment Plan and funded under the 20% Local Development Fund during the year the projects are planned to be executed in order that the constituents of the Municipality can immediately benefit from the projects.
observations.managementAction: Directed the MPDO to monitor the implementation of the PPAs.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: The Municipal Accountant is enjoined to effect the transfer of unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment with total cost of P6,162,982.00 to Other Assets account. Likewise, the Municipality is required to create a commitice or body to undertake the disposition of all unserviceable properties in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is enjoined to effect the transfer of unserviceable Property, Plant and Equipment with total cost of P6,162,982.00 to Other Assets account.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: For a systematic and effective review of transactions, we recommended that copies of Purchase Orders, together with its supporting documents, be transmitted to the Audit Team within the period prescribed under COA Circular No. 96-010.
observations.managementAction: Directed concerned personnel for the submission to the Office of the Auditor copies of Purchase Orders and Contracts.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: Strict compliance with aforementioned roles and regulations is highly enjoined.  To ensure prompt inspection of deliveries, agency official responsible for accepting and inspecting deliveries should, within twenty-four (24) hours from such acceptance/inspection, notify the auditor of the time and date  of  scheduled deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Directed concerned personnel for the submission to the Office of the Auditor copy of Acceptance and Inspection Reports.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: To avoid exceptions in audit, the Municipal Accountant is directed not to process and approve claims without the required documents. Likewise, the Municipal Accountant is required to comply with the deficiency noted and submit the required documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant is directed not to process and approve claims without the required documents and to comply with the deficiency noted and submit the required documents.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Budget Officer is enjoined t0 incorporate in the Municipality’s amount for Personal Services the amount/ rate of the hazard allowance as prescribed in RA. No. 7305 and COA Circular No. 2013-003; b. The Municipal Health Officer is required to refund the excess hazard allowance of $29,107.50; and c. Henccforth, payment of hazard allowance should be in accordance with the rate set forth in R.A. No. 7305 and COA Circular No. 2013-003.
observations.managementAction: The Budget Officer has already incorporated in the Municipality’s budget for Personal Services the amount/ rate of the hazard allowance.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel made an appeal for the disallowed amount.

observations.recommendation: We enjoined the review and approval of the GAD Plan and Budget by the Regional Office of the DILG as required by Item 7 - C6 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum  Circular No. 2013-01.
observations.managementAction: Management implement recommendation.
observations.reason: No action was taken by the Concerned personnel did no make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the following: a. The Municipal Treasurer exert more effort to collect the receivables by sending collection letters to the named creditors specially those  still living and  with capacity to pay. b. With the help of the MSWO, MAO, conducts a thorough verification and validation of creditors who are  already deceased and incapacitated to pay. c. Request for write-off of accounts receivables from concerned government agency in order to reflect the actval/ correct loan receivables  of  the municipality.
observations.managementAction: No action . was taken by the Management to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Assessor exert effort to locate all necessary documents for land which were donated and acquired thra purchase and pursue the titling of the properties in the name of the municipality to confirm its ownership.

We also recommended that the Municipal Assessor and Municipal Accountant should reconcile their records in order to identify which of the land are not included in the list and make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct value _{-of the land in the financial -statements.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor is still in the process of locating the necessary documents to comply fully with the recommendation.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We. recommended. to_the Municipal Mayor and Municipal Accountant not to process and approve claims without the required documents as provided for in the above-cited provision of laws to avoid suspension and or disallowance of the said transactions. Furthermore, the OIC-Municipal Accountant is required to comply with the deficiencies noted and submit the documents required.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed the Municipal Accountant not to process and approve claims without the required documents and to comply with the deficiencies noted.
observations.reason: Concerned personnel did not make any comment on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to require the procurement of office supplies from_the PSDBM in compliance with Section 53.5 of RIRR of Republic Act No. 9184 in order to obtain reasonable prices advantageous to the municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Mayor directed concerned personnel to make procurements from PSDBM.
observations.reason: Some items were not available at the PSDBM.
